metric_value: interBiz will deliver BizWorks via CA professional services and partners in custom applications aimed at competitive-edge new business processes.
BizWorks positioned as creating a NEW CLASS of business applications rather than competing with existing EAI (data/process automation) products.
Framework (cross-platform messaging); Agents (real-time business-condition collection/analysis); Visualization; Neugents (pattern recognition); Integration with Unicenter TNG/Jasmine.
BizWorks applications/agents will operate on a wide variety of platforms, easing B2B interoperability issues.
Neugents analyze business-data conditions over time and optimize knowledge-worker analysis and decision-making; alert on unusual patterns; predict future conditions.

interBiz Solutions was a short-lived division; BizWorks did not emerge as the durable integration platform CA envisioned; EAI category was displaced by web services, SOA, and API-economy integration through the 2000s.

BizWorks framework was superseded and effectively discontinued as a named product line within a few years of launch; CA reorganized interBiz and refocused integration strategy around more general Unicenter extensions.

Neugents remained a niche pattern-recognition technology within CA's systems-management stack; did not become the pervasive 'predictive agents' layer the 1999 profile implied, but the intellectual thread (predictive analytics on telemetry data) later matured into mainstream AIOps.
EAI = Enterprise Application Integration, covering data/process-automation products that tie existing applications together; BizWorks framed as orthogonal (new-class) rather than competitive.
CA can spread its multi-billion-dollar technology investments across many customers, lowering unit costs.
CA better suited to keeping BizWorks technology current with expected torrent of technology changes.
Implementing the functional equivalent of BizWorks would require years, assuming it were even possible.
BizWorks or BizWorks agents can be installed at modest cost with no intrusion at suppliers and customers; provides data translation, management, and security through the extended supply chain.
Business-to-Business; Prediction (via Neugents); Management Intelligence; visualization.
As global business accelerates to 'Internet speed', the ability to foresee emerging opportunities and respond intelligently becomes a true competitive advantage.
